title: CEO Commentary on Loan Condition – As of Q1'26
content:
The credit union's loan portfolio remains actively managed and appropriately reserved, though overall loan balances declined -8.4% from the previous year end. Total loans of $28,710 thousand remain concentrated in Commercial Loans/Lines of Credit ($16,422) and First Lien Real Estate ($2,799), which continue to represent the primary sources of both earnings and credit risk.

Portfolio Composition
The credit union's loan portfolio continues to be primarily concentrated in Commercial Loans/Lines of Credit. As of Q1'26, Used Vehicle totaled $479 thousand (1.7%) and First Lien Real Estate totaled $2,799 thousand (9.7%), together representing approximately 11.4% of the total portfolio.

Portfolio Composition
All Other Secured Non-Real Estate Loans/Lines of Credit accounted for $301 thousand (1.1%). Overall loan balances declined -8.4% from the previous period, reflecting continued balance-sheet contraction.

Delinquency & Credit Quality
Credit performance remains manageable despite modest delinquency levels within certain segments. Total delinquent loans fell to a negligible amount as of Q1'26, representing a decrease from the previous period. The largest delinquency exposure remains in Commercial Loans/Lines of Credit ($0) and First Lien Real Estate ($0).

Delinquency & Credit Quality
The concentration of delinquencies within commercial segments remains consistent with the overall portfolio composition. Management continues to monitor delinquency migration trends and collection performance to limit future credit deterioration.
